title: santiria kingii
intro: Santiria kingii, also known as the King's Mantle, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical rainforests of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Santiria kingii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, while also being used to treat wounds and skin infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, and is also used in traditional folk medicine in various parts of Asia.
intro_study: According to "Pharmacognosy magazine", santiria kingii has antilithiatic and diuretic potential in ethylene glycol and sodium oxalate induced urolithiasis in rats, providing structural and functional protection to the kidneys against stone formation.
